name: REPLACE_plugin_name_core_smoke
description: Must-pass fundamentals for REPLACE-plugin-name. Derive tests from the plugin's use cases — one test per behavioral contract, phrased the way a real user would ask. Keep this suite mechanical: llm_judge only as advisory. See docs/plugin-evals.md for the schema, assertion reference, and authoring checklist.

allowed_tools: REPLACE: derive from the skills' allowed-tools frontmatter, e.g.

tests.description: Cheapest possible check that the CLI, auth, and --plugin-dir boot at all. Keep as-is.
REPLACE: one core use case. Invoke the skill exactly as a user would.

assertions.rubric: REPLACE: qualitative bar, e.g. 'response is a usable draft, not meta-commentary about drafting'

tests.description: REPLACE: a skill that writes files. Assertions run against the temp copy of the fixture workspace after the turn. Pitfall: if the skill asks for confirmation before writing, a single-turn file_exists is a guaranteed false negative — use a two-turn shared conversation like the pair below.
REPLACE: something the plugin must refuse or degrade gracefully on. The default isolation already hides all MCP servers, which is how you test 'dependency unavailable' behavior.
tests.input: REPLACE: input that must trigger the guardrail

assertions.value: (?i)REPLACE fabrication marker that must not appear

tests.description: REPLACE (optional): first turn of a flow. Turns sharing 'shared:<group>' resume one Claude Code session, in one shared workspace copy, in file order. If this turn fails, later turns are skipped.

assertions.value: (?i)REPLACE: proof the skill asked before acting

tests.input: Yes — go ahead exactly as shown.
